texto: He'd learned pretty early that the illusioned virtuous creatures were no more than that— illusions. Innocence was a community that was used up and consumed fairly quickly. More often than not, innocence was just ignorance, and the true character revealed itself before long— twisted and greedy like the rest.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Camille Oster: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a sabedoria. O trecho “He'd learned pretty early that the illusioned virtuous creatures were no more than that— illusion…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, character, innocence-vs-experience pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de innocence-vs-experience.
